1	\chapter{{\v C}ech computation of higher direct images $R^i f_*$ (unconditional)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean
4	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAcyclic.lean
5	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/PresheafCech.lean
6	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FreePresheafComplex.lean
7	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechBridge.lean
8	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean
9	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
10	% quasi-coherent sheaves and \S Quasi-coherence of higher direct images
11	% (Lemmas \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial},
12	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero},
13	% \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent},
14	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}) (read from
15	% references/stacks-coherent.tex).
16	
17	\section{Setup and motivation}
18	
19	This chapter constructs the higher derived direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) for
20	\(i \geq 1\) \emph{without appealing to injective resolutions in the category of
21	sheaves of modules}. The companion development of
22	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage} defines \(R^i f_*\) as a right
23	derived functor via injective resolutions, which requires the ambient category of
24	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to have enough injectives. The {\v C}ech approach
25	developed here sidesteps this requirement:
26	it computes \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as the cohomology of an explicit complex built
27	from the pushforwards of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the finite intersections of an affine
28	open cover, and so produces an \emph{unconditional} construction of \(R^i f_*\) for
29	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) and separated quasi-compact \(f\).
30	
31	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
32	separated (so that all finite intersections of an affine open cover of \(X\) are
33	again affine), and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. We
34	write \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) for the category of quasi-coherent
35	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. A base change of \(f\) along a morphism \(g : S' \to S\)
36	is recorded by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
37	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
38	\[
39	  \begin{array}{ccc}
40	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
41	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
42	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
43	  \end{array}
44	\]
45	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), \(g' : X' \to X\) is the first
46	projection, and \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the pullback of
47	\(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
48	
49	\section{The {\v C}ech nerve and {\v C}ech complex of an affine cover}
50	
51	\begin{definition}
52	
53	\leanok
54	[{\v C}ech nerve of an affine open cover]
55	  \label{def:cech_nerve}
56	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve}
57	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:push_pull_functor, def:cech_nerve_cosimplicial, lem:push_pull_id, lem:push_pull_comp}
58	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
59	  % quasi-coherent sheaves, opening paragraph and Lemma
60	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}
61	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L36--67).
62	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
63	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an affine open.
64	  % Recall that a {\it standard open covering} of $U$ is a covering
65	  % of the form $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$
66	  % where $f_1, \ldots, f_n \in \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)$ generate
67	  % the unit ideal, see
68	  % Schemes, Definition \ref{schemes-definition-standard-covering}."
69	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
70	  quasi-coherent sheaves.}
71	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
72	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite open cover of \(X\) by
73	  affine opens, with index set \(I\). For a finite nonempty tuple
74	  \(i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I\) write
75	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = U_{i_0} \cap \cdots \cap U_{i_p}\) for the corresponding
76	  intersection. The \emph{{\v C}ech nerve} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the augmented
77	  \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) whose object in
78	  cosimplicial degree \(p\) is the direct image, along the open immersions
79	  \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\), of the restriction
80	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (or of any chosen quasi-coherent sheaf) to the
81	  \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
82	  \[
83	    [p] \;\longmapsto\;
84	      \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
85	        (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_* \,\bigl(\,\cdot\,|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
86	  \]
87	  with cofaces given by the restriction maps that omit one index and
88	  codegeneracies by the maps that repeat one index; the augmentation in degree
89	  \(-1\) is the object on all of \(X\). The variance is essential: the geometric
90	  nerve of the cover (the iterated fibre powers of \(\coprod_i U_i\) over \(X\),
91	  introduced in \S\ref{sec:cech_three_part} below) is a \emph{simplicial} object
92	  in \emph{schemes}, but applying the covariant direct-image functor turns it into
93	  a \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\); consequently the
94	  associated alternating-coface complex is a \emph{cochain} complex (differentials
95	  raising degree), not a chain complex. When \(X\) is separated each intersection
96	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is itself affine. The local affine model is the
97	  \emph{standard open covering} \(U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) of an affine open
98	  \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) by basic opens, where \(f_1, \ldots, f_n \in A\)
99	  generate the unit ideal; on such a cover the intersections are again basic opens
100	  \(D(f_{i_0} \cdots f_{i_p})\).
101	\end{definition}
102	
103	\begin{definition}
104	
105	\leanok
106	[{\v C}ech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf]
107	  \label{def:cech_complex}
108	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex}
109	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, def:relative_cech_complex_of_nerve}
110	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
111	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof)
112	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L59--77).
113	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
114	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
115	  % is identified with the complex
116	  % $$
117	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
118	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
119	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
120	  % \ldots
121	  % $$
122	  % We are asked to show that the extended complex
123	  % \begin{equation}
124	  % \label{equation-extended}
125	  % 0 \to
126	  % M \to
127	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
128	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
129	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
130	  % \ldots
131	  % \end{equation}
132	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
133	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact."
134	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
135	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover {\v C}ech
136	  vanishing).}
137	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
138	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with
139	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) affine (e.g.\ \(X\) separated). The
140	  \emph{{\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
141	  is the cochain complex obtained by applying the global-sections functor to the
142	  {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\);
143	  in degree \(p\) it is
144	  \[
145	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
146	      = \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
147	        \mathcal{F}\bigl(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
148	  \]
149	  with differential \(d : \check{\mathcal{C}}^p \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^{p+1}\) the
150	  alternating sum \((d s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
151	  s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}\) of the
152	  restriction maps. Over an affine \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) with
153	  \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) and a standard cover by the
154	  \(D(f_i)\), this is the complex
155	  \(\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to \cdots\) of
156	  localisations. The \emph{relative {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet
157	  (\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) for \(f : X \to S\) is the analogous complex in
158	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) obtained by applying \(f_*\) over each finite
159	  intersection instead of global sections; its terms are
160	  \(\prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_*
161	  \bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr)\), and it is a complex in
162	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) because each \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine and the
163	  pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated
164	  morphism is quasi-coherent.
165	\end{definition}
166	
167	\subsection{The three-part construction of the relative {\v C}ech complex}
168	\label{sec:cech_three_part}
169	
170	It is convenient to factor the construction of
171	Definitions~\ref{def:cech_nerve}--\ref{def:cech_complex} into three independent
172	pieces. Two of them are purely formal and unconditional; the sole non-trivial
173	mathematical content is isolated in the middle piece.
174	
175	\paragraph{(1) Geometric backbone — the augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow.}
176	Package the affine cover as a single morphism
177	\(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) (the canonical map out of the
178	coproduct induced by the inclusions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\)). The augmented
179	{\v C}ech nerve of this arrow is an augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
180	\(X\): its object in simplicial degree \(p\) is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
181	\[
182	  \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
183	    \;=\;
184	    \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
185	      U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
186	\]
187	with the augmentation the cover map to \(X\). Because the \(U_i\) are open
188	subschemes of \(X\), each fibre product \(U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p}\)
189	is canonically the intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). This backbone exists
190	\emph{unconditionally}: it uses only that the category of schemes has coproducts
191	and finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the {\v C}ech nerve of an
192	arrow). It carries no sheaf-theoretic data yet — it is the simplicial diagram of
193	spaces over which the construction is indexed.
194	
195	\paragraph{(2) Push--pull functor — lifting the backbone to modules.}
196	The geometric backbone is turned into a cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module by
197	the \emph{relative-direct-image functor on the over-category}
198	\[
199	  G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
200	  \qquad
201	  (Y \xrightarrow{\,p\,} X) \;\longmapsto\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
202	\]
203	sending an \(X\)-scheme \(p : Y \to X\) to the pushforward along \(p\) of the
204	pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\). Composing \(G\) with the (opposite of the) simplicial
205	backbone of (1) produces exactly the augmented cosimplicial object of
206	Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: on the degree-\(p\) piece
207	\(p : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\) one has
208	\(p_* p^*\mathcal{F} = (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\),
209	and a morphism of \(X\)-schemes \(h : (Y_1, p_1) \to (Y_2, p_2)\) (so
210	\(h \circ p_2 = p_1\)\,, reading \(p\) as the structure map) induces the
211	restriction comparison \(G(p_2) \to G(p_1)\) via the unit/counit of the
212	pull-push adjunction together with the over-triangle identifying the two
213	structure maps. This functor \(G\) is the \emph{one non-formal step} in the whole
214	construction. Its functoriality --- that \(G\) respects identities and composition
215	of morphisms over \(X\) --- is precisely a statement about the coherence of the
216	comparison isomorphisms \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) and
217	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\) for pushforward and pullback of sheaves of
218	modules, together with their compatibility with the adjunction units. Concretely,
219	pullback and pushforward of sheaves of modules are organised as a
220	pseudofunctor \(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to
221	\mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\) (sending a morphism to the adjoint pair
222	\(p^* \dashv p_*\)), and the comparison isomorphisms
223	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\), \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) are its structure
224	\(2\)-cells. The functoriality of \(G\) needs exactly three facts: the mate identity
225	expressing the conjugate of the inverse pullback comparison as the pushforward
226	comparison, its normalisation at the identity morphism, and the pseudofunctor
227	unitor and pentagon (associativity) coherences. The laws are nonetheless not formal
228	trivialities: each is a multi-step mate calculation that transports the comparison
229	isomorphisms along the over-triangle \(g \circ p_2 = p_1\) of structure maps and
230	past the adjunction unit, transporting along the over-triangle equality; the
231	obstacle is the bookkeeping of those transports, not a missing geometric ingredient.
232	
233	\paragraph{(3) Coherence-free plumbing — from the nerve to the cochain complex.}
234	The passage from the augmented cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module of (1)--(2)
235	to the relative {\v C}ech complex in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) is again purely
236	formal and uses \emph{no} pushforward/pullback coherence. One forgets the
237	augmentation, pushes the cosimplicial object forward along \(f : X \to S\) (a
238	covariant operation applied degreewise), and takes the alternating-coface-map
239	cochain complex of the resulting cosimplicial object of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules.
240	This step relies only on the preadditivity of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\): the
241	alternating sum \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j d^j\) of the cofaces is defined in any
242	preadditive category and squares to zero by the cosimplicial identities. Thus the
243	relative {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
244	of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} is genuinely \emph{defined} in terms of the
245	{\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: once the nerve is constructed,
246	the complex follows with no further input. In summary, of the three pieces only
247	the push--pull functor (2) requires content beyond formal category theory, and
248	that content is the pseudofunctor coherence of pushforward and pullback: the
249	comparison \(2\)-cells of the pseudofunctor
250	\(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to \mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\)
251	and their unitor/pentagon identities.
252	
253	\paragraph{Formal bricks of the three-part construction.}
254	The geometric backbone (1), the push--pull functor (2), and the plumbing (3) are
255	recorded as the following declarations. The backbone and plumbing are
256	unconditional; the push--pull object and morphism maps are likewise unconditional
257	bricks, and the functor laws assembling them are the lone coherence statement.
258	
259	\begin{definition}\leanok
260	[Arrow of an affine cover]
261	  \label{def:cover_arrow}
262	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrow}
263	  Let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be an affine open cover. Its
264	  \emph{cover arrow} is the single morphism
265	  \(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) obtained as the universal map out of
266	  the coproduct induced by the open immersions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\). Packaging
267	  the cover as one arrow lets the generic {\v C}ech-nerve-of-an-arrow machinery
268	  apply.
269	\end{definition}
270	
271	\begin{definition}[Augmented {\v C}ech nerve of a cover]
272	  \label{def:cover_cech_nerve}
273	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerve, AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerveOver, AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerveOverAug}
274	  \uses{def:cover_arrow}
275	  The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of the cover arrow of
276	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_arrow}: the augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
277	  \(X\) whose degree-\(p\) object is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
278	  \[
279	    \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
280	      = \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
281	        U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
282	  \]
283	  with augmentation the cover map to \(X\). It exists unconditionally because the
284	  category of schemes has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the
285	  {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow). This is the geometric backbone (1).
286	\end{definition}
287	
288	\begin{definition}\leanok
289	[Push--pull object map]
290	  \label{def:push_pull_obj}
291	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullObj}
292	  The object map of the push--pull functor \(G\): to an \(X\)-scheme
293	  \(p : Y \to X\) it assigns
294	  \[
295	    G(Y, p) \;=\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
296	  \]
297	  the pushforward along \(p\) of the pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\).
298	\end{definition}
299	
300	\begin{definition}[Push--pull comparison map]
301	  \label{def:push_pull_map}
302	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_eq_raw, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_self, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_self_gen, AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardComp_hom_app_id, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_unit_comp}
303	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj}
304	  The morphism map of \(G\). A morphism \(g : (Y_2, p_2) \to (Y_1, p_1)\) of
305	  \(X\)-schemes has underlying map \(\bar g : Y_2 \to Y_1\) satisfying the
306	  over-triangle \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\); the contravariant functor produces the
307	  restriction comparison \(G(Y_1, p_1) \to G(Y_2, p_2)\) as the five-step composite
308	  \[
309	    p_{1*}\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
310	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{1*}(\eta)\,}
311	    p_{1*}\,\bar g_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
312	      \xrightarrow{\,\sim\,}
313	    (p_1 \circ \bar g)_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
314	      \;=\;
315	    p_{2*}\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
316	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{2*}(\sim)\,}
317	    p_{2*}\,p_2^*\mathcal{F},
318	  \]
319	  where \(\eta\) is the unit of the adjunction \(\bar g^* \dashv \bar g_*\), the
320	  second arrow is the pushforward comparison \(p_{1*}\bar g_* \cong (p_1 \bar g)_*\),
321	  the equality is the substitution \(p_2 = p_1 \circ \bar g\) of the over-triangle,
322	  and the last arrow applies \(p_{2*}\) to the pullback comparison
323	  \(\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (p_1 \bar g)^* = p_2^*\). The two over-triangle
324	  substitutions are realised as coercions along \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\).
325	\end{definition}
326	
327	\begin{lemma}
328	[Push--pull functor — identity law]
329	  \label{lem:push_pull_id}
330	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_id}
331	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map}
332	  The morphism map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} respects identities:
333	  \(G(\mathrm{id}_Y) = \mathrm{id}_{G(Y)}\) for every \(X\)-scheme \(Y\).
334	\end{lemma}
335	
336	\begin{proof}
337	  A direct calculation with comparison isomorphisms, with no sectionwise content.
338	  Rewrite the head \(p_*(\eta)\) followed by the pushforward comparison as the
339	  conjugate-mate of the inverse pullback comparison; the identity leg then collapses
340	  by the identity normalisation of the pseudofunctor together with right unitality,
341	  and the two over-triangle transport coercions cancel against the unitality coercion.
342	\end{proof}
343	
344	\begin{lemma}
345	[Push--pull functor — composition law]
346	  \label{lem:push_pull_comp}
347	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_comp, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_comp, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_pentagon}
348	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map, lem:push_pull_unit_mate, lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
349	  The morphism map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} is contravariantly functorial:
350	  for composable morphisms \(h : Y_3 \to Y_2\), \(g : Y_2 \to Y_1\) of \(X\)-schemes,
351	  \[
352	    G(g \circ h) = G(h) \circ G(g).
353	  \]
354	  Together with Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_id} this assembles
355	  Definitions~\ref{def:push_pull_obj}--\ref{def:push_pull_map} into a functor
356	  \(G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \to \operatorname{QCoh}(X)\); composing \(G\) with
357	  the backbone of Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} yields the {\v C}ech nerve of
358	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}.
359	\end{lemma}
360	
361	\begin{proof}
362	  \uses{lem:push_pull_unit_mate, lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
363	  The composition identity \(G(g \circ h) = G(h) \circ G(g)\) follows from three
364	  coherence facts. First, the composite adjunction unit \(\eta^{g \circ h}\) decomposes into
365	  the iterated units \(\eta^{g}\), \(\eta^{h}\) via the mate identity of
366	  Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_unit_mate} and the naturality of the adjunction unit. Second,
367	  the resulting pullback-side identity is the \emph{pentagon coherence} of the pullback
368	  pseudofunctor — the associativity \(2\)-cell relating the two bracketings of the comparison
369	  isomorphism \((p \circ q \circ r)^* \cong r^* q^* p^*\). Third, the over-triangle transport
370	  coherences are absorbed by Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}.
371	\end{proof}
372	
373	\begin{lemma}
374	[Base-change unit (mate) identity for the push--pull head]
375	  \label{lem:push_pull_unit_mate}
376	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_unit_mate}
377	  \uses{def:push_pull_map}
378	  For composable scheme morphisms \(f : A \to B\), \(p : B \to Z\) and a
379	  \(\mathcal{O}_Z\)-module \(N\), the adjunction unit \(\eta^p_N\) of
380	  \(p^* \dashv p_*\) at \(N\), followed by \(p_*\) of the unit \(\eta^f\) for
381	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) at \(p^*N\), followed by the pushforward comparison
382	  \(p_* f_* \cong (f \circ p)_*\), equals the unit \(\eta^{f \circ p}_N\) for the
383	  composite followed by \((f \circ p)_*\) applied to the inverse pullback
384	  comparison \((f \circ p)^* \cong f^* p^*\):
385	  \[
386	    \eta^p_N \;\circ\; p_*(\eta^f_{p^*N}) \;\circ\;
387	      \bigl(p_* f_* \cong (f \circ p)_*\bigr)
388	    \;=\;
389	    \eta^{f \circ p}_N \;\circ\; (f \circ p)_*\bigl((f\circ p)^* \cong f^* p^*\bigr)^{-1}.
390	  \]
391	  This is the mate-calculus core that converts the single-morphism unit
392	  \(\eta^{f \circ p}\) into the iterated units \(\eta^p, \eta^f\); it is the
393	  reusable ingredient consumed by the functoriality (pentagon) law of the
394	  push--pull comparison map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map}.
395	\end{lemma}
396	
397	\begin{proof}
398	  A direct mate-calculus computation: unfold both sides using the definition of
399	  the adjunction unit for the composite and the pseudofunctor structure maps, then
400	  verify the equality by the mate identity for the pushforward/pullback comparison.
401	\end{proof}
402	
403	\begin{lemma}
404	[Over-triangle transport cancellation for the push--pull tail]
405	  \label{lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
406	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_transport_cancel}
407	  \uses{def:push_pull_map}
408	  Let \(\bar g : Y_2 \to Y_1\), \(p_1 : Y_1 \to X\), \(p_2 : Y_2 \to X\) satisfy the
409	  over-triangle \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\), and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a
410	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then the pullback-comparison leg of the push--pull map,
411	  conjugated by the two \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) coercions along the over-triangle,
412	  collapses to the transport-light form
413	  \[
414	    e_1 \;\circ\; p_{2*}\bigl((\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (\bar g p_1)^*)_{\mathcal{F}}\bigr)
415	      \;\circ\; e_2
416	    \;=\;
417	    (\bar g p_1)_*\bigl((\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (\bar g p_1)^*)_{\mathcal{F}}\bigr)
418	      \;\circ\; e_3,
419	  \]
420	  where \(e_1, e_2, e_3\) are the \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) coercions induced by
421	  \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\). It states the over-triangle cancellation in the
422	  push--pull comparison map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} with the
423	  over-triangle equality as a free hypothesis, so a single substitution turns the
424	  transports into identities; it is the reusable pre-coherence brick for the
425	  composition (pentagon) law.
426	\end{lemma}
427	
428	\begin{proof}
429	  Substitute the over-triangle equality \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\) to replace
430	  transport coercions by identities, then verify the cancellation by direct
431	  calculation with the pushforward comparison.
432	\end{proof}
433	
434	\begin{definition}
435	[Push--pull functor]
436	  \label{def:push_pull_functor}
437	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullFunctor}
438	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map, lem:push_pull_id, lem:push_pull_comp}
439	  The \emph{push--pull functor}
440	  \[
441	    G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
442	  \]
443	  assembling the object map \(p \mapsto p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F}\) of
444	  Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_obj} and the comparison morphism map of
445	  Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} into a genuine functor. Functoriality is exactly the two
446	  laws established above: the identity law
447	  Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_id} and the composition law Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_comp}. The
448	  functor is contravariant in the over-category \((X/\mathbf{Sch})\); it is the lift of the
449	  geometric backbone (1) to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described as step (2) above.
450	\end{definition}
451	
452	\begin{definition}
453	[{\v C}ech nerve cosimplicial object]
454	  \label{def:cech_nerve_cosimplicial}
455	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechNerveCosimplicial}
456	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:push_pull_functor}
457	  The (non-augmented) cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module obtained by transporting the
458	  geometric {\v C}ech backbone in \((X/\mathbf{Sch})\) of
459	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} through the push--pull functor
460	  \(G\) of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_functor}: degreewise, the \(p\)-th term is the
461	  product over \((i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}\) of the \((p+1)\)-fold-intersection

463	  underlying cosimplicial object that the {\v C}ech nerve of

480	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} into the {\v C}ech complex.

483	\section{Affine acyclicity of the {\v C}ech complex}
484	
485	\begin{definition}
486	[Standard affine cover from a spanning family]
487	  \label{def:standard_affine_cover}
488	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}
489	  \mathlibok
490	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open}).}
491	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring (so \(R = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R,
492	  \mathcal{O})\)) and let \(s : \iota \to R\) be a family of elements that generate
493	  the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\). The
494	  \emph{standard affine open cover} of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) associated with
495	  \(s\) is the affine open cover whose index set is \(\iota\), whose \(i\)-th member
496	  is the basic open \(D(s_i)\), and whose \(i\)-th structure morphism is the map of
497	  affine schemes induced by the localisation homomorphism \(R \to R_{s_i}\); that
498	  is, in Mathlib notation the defining equation
499	  \(\bigl(\text{affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}\bigr)_f\, i =
500	  \operatorname{Spec.map}\bigl(\text{algebraMap}\; R\; (\operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i))\bigr)\),
501	  so that the sections over the \(i\)-th piece are the \emph{away} localisation
502	  \(R_{s_i} = \operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i)\) and the geometric piece is
503	  \(D(s_i)\). This is exactly the standard open covering
504	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R = \bigcup_i D(s_i)\) by basic opens, packaged as a single
505	  cover object. It is the spanning-family bundle \((s, hs)\) that the affine
506	  {\v C}ech vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is stated over.
507	\end{definition}

509	\subsection{The categorical--module bridge (L1)}
510	\label{sec:cech_l1_bridge}
511	
512	The affine vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is a statement about the
513	\emph{section} {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
514	\mathcal{F})\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} --- a cochain complex of
515	abelian groups --- whereas the combinatorial contracting-homotopy core operates on a
516	concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules. The passage between the two, the
517	\emph{categorical--module bridge}, decomposes into two self-contained statements: the
518	section identification (each section group is an away localisation, with the
519	restriction maps the canonical localisation maps) and the homology-to-exactness
520	reduction (vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
521	underlying localised-module complex). These are isolated as
522	Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} and
523	Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} below; the assembly --- feeding each
524	positive-degree node through the local-to-global exactness criterion and closing with
525	the dependent combinatorial port --- is carried out in the proof of
526	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.
527	
528	\begin{definition}
529	[Quasi-coherent sections over a basic open are an away localisation]
530	  \label{def:qcoh_sections_localized}
531	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}
532	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:standard_affine_cover}
533	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Lemma \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
534	  % items (4)--(5) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L701--707).
535	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "(4) For every $f\in R$ we have $\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde M) = M_f$
536	  % as an $R_f$-module.
537	  % (5) Whenever $D(g) \subset D(f)$ the restriction mappings
538	  % on $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$ and $\widetilde M$
539	  % are the maps
540	  % $R_f \to R_g$ and $M_f \to M_g$ from Lemma
541	  % \ref{lemma-standard-open}."
542	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
543	  items (4)--(5).}
544	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
545	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module, and write
546	  \(M = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})\) for its module of global
547	  sections. For every \(g \in R\), the restriction-of-sections map
548	  \(M = \mathcal{F}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g))\) exhibits the
549	  section group \(\mathcal{F}(D(g))\) as the away localisation \(M_g\) of \(M\); that
550	  is, this map is an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) for the multiplicative
551	  submonoid \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g\) of powers of \(g\). Moreover, for
552	  \(D(g_2) \subseteq D(g_1)\) the restriction map
553	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(g_1)) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g_2))\) is, under these identifications, the
554	  canonical localisation map \(M_{g_1} \to M_{g_2}\) (the unique \(R\)-linear map
555	  compatible with the localisation units). Item~(4) of Tag~01HV gives the first
556	  assertion for \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) (\(\Gamma(D(g), \widetilde{M}) = M_g\)
557	  as an \(R_g\)-module) and item~(5) gives the second (the restriction along
558	  \(D(g) \subset D(f)\) is the localisation map \(M_f \to M_g\)).
559	
560	  The module-level input on the side of Mathlib is the development
561	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde}: the comparison map
562	  \((\operatorname{tilde.toOpen} M\, (D(g))).\mathrm{hom}\) carries the instance
563	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g)\), and
564	  \(\operatorname{isUnit\_algebraMap\_end\_basicOpen}\) records that \(g\) acts
565	  invertibly on the sections over \(D(g)\); the restriction compatibility is
566	  \(\operatorname{tilde.toOpen\_res}\). These supply the statement \emph{verbatim} for
567	  the standard sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\). For an \emph{arbitrary} quasi-coherent
568	  \(\mathcal{F}\) the remaining step is the canonical comparison
569	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})}\), i.e.\
570	  that the counit \(\operatorname{Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism. This
571	  is \emph{not} yet available in Mathlib for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\):
572	  Mathlib provides it only from a \emph{global} presentation
573	  (\(\operatorname{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}\)), whereas quasi-coherence
574	  supplies only \emph{local} presentation data on a cover. Globalising the local data
575	  on the affine \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) --- the affine equivalence
576	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\), Stacks
577	  Tag~01I8 --- is the one genuine gap this definition records as a build target; it is
578	  the canonical missing affine equivalence and is self-contained.
579	\end{definition}

617	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to

622	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (identification with the

624	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
625	  R}\)-module and \(\mathcal{U}\) the standard cover associated with a spanning family
626	  \(s : \iota \to R\). Under the section identification of

628	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
629	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} is isomorphic, as a cochain complex, to
630	  the concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules
631	  \[
632	    D^\bullet : \quad
633	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
634	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0,1\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
635	    \cdots,
636	    \qquad
637	    s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
638	  \]
639	  whose differential is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation maps. For
640	  each \(p\), vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
641	  underlying \(R\)-module maps,
642	  \[
643	    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
644	      \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr)
645	    \;\Longleftrightarrow\;
646	    \operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\bigr).
647	  \]
648	  The two directions are supplied by Mathlib: \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the
649	  homology is \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) at \(p\)
650	  (\(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\)), and \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)
651	  of a short complex of \(R\)-modules is \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the
652	  underlying linear maps (\(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\)).
653	\end{lemma}
654	
655	\begin{proof}
656	  \uses{def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_complex}
657	  The term identification is the degreewise statement of
658	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the degree-\(p\) section group
659	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\), since
660	  each \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap
661	  D(s_{\sigma(p)})\) is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
662	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\) and the sections there are the away
663	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The differential identification is item~(5) of
664	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: each face restriction
665	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the
666	  canonical localisation map \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\), so the
667	  alternating sum of restrictions is the alternating sum of localisation maps, the
668	  differential of \(D^\bullet\). This gives the isomorphism of cochain complexes
669	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \cong D^\bullet\). The
670	  homology equivalence then follows by transporting along this isomorphism and
671	  applying \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) (\(\operatorname{IsZero}\)
672	  homology \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)) and
673	  \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\) (\(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of
674	  \(R\)-modules \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\)).
675	
676	  The identification of the abstract section complex with the concrete localised
677	  complex \(D^\bullet\) --- together with the construction of the prepend transport
678	  \(c\) and the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\) discharging the unit, shift, and
679	  coface-commutation compatibilities \(\mathtt{hu}\)/\(\mathtt{hsh}\)/\(\mathtt{hcomm}\)
680	  of the dependent combinatorial port --- is the bulk of the bridge. The non-circular
681	  route is reflection along the localisation equivalence on its essential image (the
682	  global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is the inverse equivalence there, so it reflects
683	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of homology); it is \emph{not} the assertion that
684	  ``\(\Gamma\) preserves homology'', which would presuppose the very affine exactness
685	  being proved.
686	\end{proof}

module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.Basic

/-!
# Localizations of modules away from an element
-/

@[expose] public section

/-- Given `x : R` and `f : M →ₗ[R] M'`, `IsLocalization.Away x f` states that `M'`
  is isomorphic to the localization of `M` at the submonoid generated by `x`. -/
protected abbrev IsLocalizedModule.Away {R M M' : Type*} [CommSemiring R] (x : R) [AddCommMonoid M]
    [Module R M] [AddCommMonoid M'] [Module R M'] (f : M →ₗ[R] M') :=
  IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers x) f

/-- Given `x : R`, `LocalizedModule.Away x M` is the localization of `M` at the
  submonoid generated by `x`. -/
protected abbrev LocalizedModule.Away {R : Type*} [CommSemiring R] (x : R)
    (M : Type*) [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] :=
  LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers x) M

lemma IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars (S : Submonoid R)
    {N : Type*} [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R N] [Module A M] [Module A N]
    [IsScalarTower R A M] [IsScalarTower R A N]
    (f : M →ₗ[A] N) [IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R)] :
    IsLocalizedModule (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f where
  map_units x := by
    obtain ⟨_, x, hx, rfl⟩ := x
    have := IsLocalizedModule.map_units (f.restrictScalars R) ⟨x, hx⟩
    simp only [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply, Module.End.isUnit_iff] at this ⊢
    exact this
  surj y := by
    obtain ⟨⟨x, t⟩, e⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj S (f.restrictScalars R) y
    exact ⟨⟨x, ⟨_, t, t.2, rfl⟩⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def] using e⟩
  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} e := by
    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := S) (f := f.restrictScalars R) e
    refine ⟨⟨_, c, c.2, rfl⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def]⟩

lemma IsLocalizedModule.of_exists_mul_mem {N : Type*} [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R N]
    (S T : Submonoid R) (h : S ≤ T) (h' : ∀ x : T, ∃ m : R, m * x ∈ S)
    (f : M →ₗ[R] N) [IsLocalizedModule S f] :
    IsLocalizedModule T f where
  map_units x := by
    obtain ⟨m, mx⟩ := h' x
    have := IsLocalizedModule.map_units f ⟨_, mx⟩
    rw [map_mul, (Algebra.commute_algebraMap_left _ _).isUnit_mul_iff] at this
    exact this.2
  surj y := by
    obtain ⟨⟨x, t⟩, e⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj S f y
    exact ⟨⟨x, ⟨t, h t.2⟩⟩, e⟩
  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} e := by
    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := S) (f := f) e
    exact ⟨⟨c, h c.2⟩, hc⟩

namespace IsLocalizedModule

variable [IsLocalizedModule S f]

/-- If `(M', f : M ⟶ M')` satisfies universal property of localized module, there is a canonical
map `LocalizedModule S M ⟶ M'`.
-/
noncomputable def fromLocalizedModule' : LocalizedModule S M → M' := fun p =>
  p.liftOn (fun x => (IsLocalizedModule.map_units f x.2).unit⁻¹.val (f x.1))

        Module.End.algebraMap_isUnit_inv_apply_eq_iff', ← map_smul]
      exact (IsLocalizedModule.eq_iff_exists S f).mpr ⟨c, eq1.symm⟩)

  rfl

/-- A power of an invertibly-acting element acts injectively on a localisation:
the scalar-cancellation device behind localisation transitivity. -/
lemma Inverts.smul_pow_cancel {a : R} {N : Type u} [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
    (ha : Inverts a N) (n : ℕ) {p q : N} (h : a ^ n • p = a ^ n • q) : p = q := by
  have hu : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R N) (a ^ n)) := by
    rw [map_pow]; exact ha.pow n
  apply ((Module.End.isUnit_iff _).mp hu).injective
  simpa only [Module.algebraMap_end_eq_smul_id, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.id_coe,
    id_eq] using h

/-- **Localisation transitivity for the away comparison.** If `Ma` localises `M`
away from `a` and `Mb` away from `a * b`, the comparison map `Ma → Mb` exhibits
`Mb` as the localisation of `Ma` away from `b` — the "localisation of a
localisation" identity `M_a[1/b] = M_{ab}`.  This is the keystone that feeds the
per-spanning-element reduction `exact_of_isLocalized_span` of the section Čech
complex: localising the un-localised coefficient `M_{s_σ}` at a spanning element
`s_r` yields the doubly-localised coefficient `M_{s_r · s_σ}` of `cechCoeff`. -/
lemma comparison_isLocalizedModule {a b : R}
    {Ma Mb : Type u} [AddCommGroup Ma] [Module R Ma] [AddCommGroup Mb] [Module R Mb]
    (fa : M →ₗ[R] Ma) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) fa]
    (fb : M →ₗ[R] Mb) [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (a * b)) fb]
    (hb : Inverts a Mb) :
    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b) (comparison fa fb hb) := by
  have hbB : Inverts b Mb := Inverts.of_dvd ⟨a, by rw [mul_comm]⟩ fb
  have haA : Inverts a Ma := Inverts.of_dvd dvd_rfl fa
  refine ⟨fun x => hbB.isUnit_powers x, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- surjectivity
    intro y
    obtain ⟨⟨m, u⟩, hu⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers (a * b)) fb y
    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := u.2
    refine ⟨⟨IsLocalizedModule.mk' fa m ⟨a ^ n, n, rfl⟩, ⟨b ^ n, n, rfl⟩⟩, ?_⟩
    apply hb.smul_pow_cancel n
    -- RHS: a^n • comparison (mk' fa m ⟨a^n⟩) = fb m
    have hz : (a ^ n : R) • IsLocalizedModule.mk' fa m (⟨a ^ n, n, rfl⟩ : Submonoid.powers a)
        = fa m := by
      have h := IsLocalizedModule.mk'_cancel' fa m (⟨a ^ n, n, rfl⟩ : Submonoid.powers a)
      rwa [Submonoid.smul_def] at h
    rw [Submonoid.smul_def, ← map_smul, hz, comparison_apply]
    -- LHS: a^n • (b^n • y) = fb m
    rw [smul_smul, ← mul_pow, ← hn, ← Submonoid.smul_def]
    exact hu
  · -- exists_of_eq
    intro x₁ x₂ e
    set w := x₁ - x₂ with hw
    have hcw : comparison fa fb hb w = 0 := by rw [hw, map_sub, e, sub_self]
    obtain ⟨⟨m, s⟩, hs⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Submonoid.powers a) fa w
    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := s.2
    have hsw : a ^ k • w = fa m := by rw [hk, ← Submonoid.smul_def]; exact hs
    have hfbm : fb m = 0 := by
      have h1 : comparison fa fb hb (fa m) = 0 := by
        rw [← hsw, map_smul, hcw, smul_zero]
      rwa [comparison_apply] at h1
    obtain ⟨d, hd⟩ := (IsLocalizedModule.eq_iff_exists (Submonoid.powers (a * b)) fb).mp
      (by rw [hfbm, map_zero])
    obtain ⟨j, hj⟩ := d.2
    have hdm : (a * b) ^ j • m = 0 := by
      rw [hj, ← Submonoid.smul_def, hd, smul_zero]
    -- a^{j+k} • (b^j • w) = 0
    have hzero : a ^ (j + k) • (b ^ j • w) = a ^ (j + k) • (0 : Ma) := by
      rw [smul_zero, smul_smul, pow_add, mul_comm (a ^ j) (a ^ k), mul_assoc, ← mul_pow,
        mul_comm (a ^ k), ← mul_assoc, ← pow_add]
      -- goal: (a^k * (a*b)^j) • w = 0 ; use hsw and hdm
      rw [mul_comm, mul_smul, hsw, ← map_smul, hdm, map_zero]
    have hbw : b ^ j • w = 0 := haA.smul_pow_cancel (j + k) hzero
    refine ⟨⟨b ^ j, j, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
    rw [Submonoid.smul_def, Submonoid.smul_def]
    rw [hw, smul_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hbw
    exact hbw

end AwayComparison

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the un-localised section Čech module complex `D•`

`D^m` is the product `∏_{σ : Fin m → ι} M_{s_σ}` of away-localisations of
`M = Γ(Spec R, F)` at the multi-index products `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}`; its differential
is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation comparison maps (index deletion
drops a factor, so `s_{σ∘dⱼ} ∣ s_σ`).  This is the concrete `R`-module complex `D•`
of `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` that the section Čech complex is identified
with.  Positive-degree exactness is reduced — via `exact_of_isLocalized_span` — to
the localised complexes already shown exact in `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`;
the bridge is the localisation-transitivity lemma
`AwayComparison.comparison_isLocalizedModule` (applied here as
`dToCech_isLocalizedModule`). -/

namespace SectionCechModule

open CechLocalized AwayComparison

variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} (s : ι → R)
variable (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]

/-- The un-localised section Čech coefficient `D_σ = M_{s_σ}`. -/
abbrev dCoeff {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) : Type u :=
  LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M

/-- The un-localised coface comparison `δ : M_{s_{σ∘dⱼ}} → M_{s_σ}` (R-linear), the
canonical localisation map for the divisibility `s_{σ∘dⱼ} ∣ s_σ`. -/
noncomputable def dCoface (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
    dCoeff s M (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →ₗ[R] dCoeff s M σ :=
  comparison
    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s (σ ∘ j.succAbove))) M)
    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M)
    (Inverts.of_dvd (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ j)
      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M))

/-- The un-localised differential `d : D^m → D^{m+1}` as an `R`-linear map: the
alternating sum of cofaces, assembled with `LinearMap.pi` over the output tuple. -/
noncomputable def dDiff (m : ℕ) :
    (∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) →ₗ[R] (∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, dCoeff s M σ) :=
  LinearMap.pi fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (m + 1),
    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • (dCoface s M m σ j ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj (σ ∘ j.succAbove))

/-- Apply form of `dDiff`: the alternating sum of cofaces of the deleted faces. -/
lemma dDiff_apply (m : ℕ) (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
    dDiff s M m t σ
      = ∑ j : Fin (m + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • dCoface s M m σ j (t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)) := by
  simp only [dDiff, LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.coeFn_sum, Finset.sum_apply,
    LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.proj_apply]

variable (r : ι)

/-- The per-index localisation comparison `M_{s_σ} → M_{s_r · s_σ}` carrying the
un-localised coefficient to the `cechCoeff` coefficient localised at `s_r`. -/
noncomputable def dToCech {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) :
    dCoeff s M σ →ₗ[R] cechCoeff s M r σ :=
  comparison
    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M)
    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)
    (Inverts.of_dvd ⟨s r, by rw [mul_comm]⟩
      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M))

/-- **Transitivity payoff**: `dToCech` exhibits the localised coefficient
`cechCoeff = M_{s_r · s_σ}` as the localisation of the un-localised coefficient
`M_{s_σ}` away from the spanning element `s_r`.  Direct from
`AwayComparison.comparison_isLocalizedModule`; this is the per-coefficient input the
`exact_of_isLocalized_span` reduction of the section Čech complex consumes. -/
lemma dToCech_isLocalizedModule {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) :
    IsLocalizedModule.Away (s r) (dToCech s M r σ) := by
  haveI inst : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ * s r))
      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M) := by
    rw [mul_comm]; infer_instance
  unfold dToCech
  exact comparison_isLocalizedModule _ _ _

end SectionCechModule

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-- **Per-coface localisation naturality**: the localised coface `cechCoface`
intertwines the per-index comparison `dToCech` with the un-localised coface

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAcyclic.lean

## Session summary (iter-019, mathlib-build, P3 L1 lane)

Built **10 axiom-clean declarations** advancing step (a) of the section-form L1
bridge — including the single hardest gap the planner flagged (**localisation
transitivity** `M_{s_σ}[1/s_r] = M_{s_r·s_σ}`). All `#print axioms` =
`{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. File compiles; sorry count **1 → 1**
(only the pre-existing superseded relative-form `CechAcyclic.affine`, line 74,
untouched).

### Added in `namespace AwayComparison` (lines ~590–680)
- `Inverts.smul_pow_cancel` — a power of an invertibly-acting element acts
  injectively on a localisation (scalar-cancellation device). AXIOM-CLEAN.
- **`comparison_isLocalizedModule`** — **KEYSTONE / localisation transitivity.**
  If `Ma` localises `M` away from `a` and `Mb` away from `a*b`, the comparison
  `Ma → Mb` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers b)`. Proved bottom-up via the
  `IsLocalizedModule.mk` constructor (map_units via `Inverts.of_dvd`; surjectivity
  via `mk'_cancel'` + unit cancellation; cancellation via `eq_iff_exists` + unit
  cancellation on both `Ma` and `Mb`). AXIOM-CLEAN. **This was the planner's named
  remaining gap** ("localisation transitivity `M →[s_σ] →[s r] = M →[s r s_σ]`").

### Added in `namespace SectionCechModule` (lines ~862–972)
The un-localised section Čech module complex `D•`:
- `dCoeff {m} (σ : Fin m → ι) : Type u := LocalizedModule (powers (sprod s σ)) M`
  (abbrev) — the coefficient `M_{s_σ}`.
- `dCoface (m) (σ) (j) : dCoeff (σ∘dⱼ) →ₗ[R] dCoeff σ` — un-localised coface
  comparison (R-linear), via `comparison` + `Inverts.of_dvd (sprod_succAbove_dvd …)`.
- `dDiff (m) : (∀σ:Fin m→ι, dCoeff σ) →ₗ[R] (∀σ:Fin (m+1)→ι, dCoeff σ)` — the
  un-localised differential as a bundled `LinearMap` (alternating sum assembled
  with `LinearMap.pi` over the output tuple, ℤ-smul of composed `LinearMap.proj`).
- `dDiff_apply` — its pointwise alternating-sum form. AXIOM-CLEAN.
- `dToCech (r) {m} (σ) : dCoeff σ →ₗ[R] cechCoeff s M r σ` — per-index comparison
  `M_{s_σ} → M_{s_r·s_σ}`.
- **`dToCech_isLocalizedModule`** — `IsLocalizedModule.Away (s r) (dToCech …)`,
  the direct payoff of the transitivity keystone (handles the `s_r·s_σ` vs
  `s_σ·s_r` commutativity by an instance `rw [mul_comm]; infer_instance`).
  AXIOM-CLEAN.
- **`cechCoface_dToCech`** — per-coface localisation-naturality square
  `cechCoface ∘ dToCech(σ∘dⱼ) = dToCech σ ∘ dCoface`, via `comparison_comp_apply`
  twice. AXIOM-CLEAN.
- **`dToCech_comm`** — **the heart of the `exact_of_isLocalized_span` reduction**:
  `depDiff (cechCoface r) (fun σ => dToCech r σ (t σ)) = fun σ => dToCech r σ (dDiff t σ)`.
  Summing `cechCoface_dToCech` over the alternating signs (uses `dDiff_apply`,
  `map_sum`, `map_zsmul`). AXIOM-CLEAN.

## Why I stopped — `Partial progress`

Real progress: **10 axiom-clean declarations** (named above). All the genuine
mathematical content of step (a) is now in place: localisation transitivity, the
per-coefficient `IsLocalizedModule.Away` instance, and the differential-naturality
square. What remains for the NAMED target `dDiff_exact` (= `lem:cech_acyclic_affine`
step a, positive-degree `Function.Exact` of `D•`) is **plumbing** around
`exact_of_isLocalized_span`, NOT new mathematics. I stopped before it to avoid
leaving a long half-built assembly (the invariant forbids `sorry`); the bricks make
it a deterministic next step.

## `dDiff_exact` (NOT ADDED — precise next-step recipe)

Target signature (add `[Finite ι]`):
```lean
lemma dDiff_exact [Finite ι] (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (m : ℕ) :
    Function.Exact (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2))
```
Apply `exact_of_isLocalized_span (Set.range s) hs Mₚ f Nₚ g Lₚ h F G H` with:
- `F := dDiff s M (m+1)`, `G := dDiff s M (m+2)`.
- For `ρ : ↑(Set.range s)`, set `iOf ρ := ρ.2.choose` (so `s (iOf ρ) = ↑ρ` by
  `ρ.2.choose_spec`); `Mₚ ρ := ∀σ:Fin (m+1)→ι, cechCoeff s M (iOf ρ) σ` (and
  `Nₚ`, `Lₚ` at `m+2`, `m+3`).
- `f ρ := LinearMap.pi (fun σ => dToCech s M (iOf ρ) σ ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj σ)`.
- Instance `IsLocalizedModule.Away ↑ρ (f ρ)`: from **`IsLocalizedModule.pi`**
  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.IsBaseChangePi`, needs `[Finite (Fin m→ι)]`,
  i.e. `[Finite ι]`) fed `dToCech_isLocalizedModule`, giving
  `IsLocalizedModule.Away (s (iOf ρ)) (f ρ)`; transport along `ρ.2.choose_spec`
  (`↑ρ = s (iOf ρ)`) to rewrite the away element.
- `H ρ : Function.Exact ⇑(map (powers ↑ρ) (f ρ) (f' ρ) F) ⇑(map (powers ↑ρ) (f' ρ) (f'' ρ) G)`.

**The one missing brick for `H ρ`** — identify `map F` with the localised
differential. Two clean options:
1. Build a **bundled** localised differential
   `locDiff (r) (m) : (∀σ:Fin m→ι, cechCoeff s M r σ) →ₗ[R] (∀σ, cechCoeff …)` by
   the SAME `LinearMap.pi`/`∑ (-1)^j • (· ∘ₗ proj)` pattern as `dDiff`, using the
   R-linear comparison underlying `cechCoface` (add a thin `cechCofaceLin` =
   the `comparison` before `.toAddMonoidHom`, with `cechCoface_apply :
   cechCoface … x = cechCofaceLin … x`). Then:
   - `⇑(locDiff r m) = CombinatorialCech.depDiff (cechCoface s M r) (m:=m)` by
     funext + a `locDiff_apply` mirroring `dDiff_apply` — so `cechLocalized_exact`
     transports to `Function.Exact (locDiff r (m+1)) (locDiff r (m+2))`.
   - `IsLocalizedModule.map (powers (s r)) (f) (f') (dDiff …) = locDiff r …` by
     **`IsLocalizedModule.ext`** (map_units from `Inverts.isUnit_powers`), checking
     `… ∘ₗ f = locDiff ∘ₗ f` — which is exactly **`dToCech_comm`** (note
     `(f ρ) t = fun σ => dToCech (iOf ρ) σ (t σ)` and `map_apply` gives
     `map F (f x) = f' (F x)`).
   - Then `H ρ` = transported `cechLocalized_exact`, with `↑ρ = s (iOf ρ)`.
2. (Alt.) Skip bundling: prove `⇑(map F) = depDiff (cechCoface (iOf ρ))` as a raw
   function equality via `IsLocalizedModule.ext` applied to the bundled `map`
   against a bundled `locDiff` (still needs option-1's `locDiff`). Option 1 is the
   cleaner factoring.

All names verified present this session: `exact_of_isLocalized_span`,
`IsLocalizedModule.pi`, `IsLocalizedModule.map`, `IsLocalizedModule.map_apply`,
`IsLocalizedModule.ext`, `IsLocalizedModule.mk'_cancel'`, `eq_iff_exists`.

## Downstream (steps b–d) — unchanged from PROGRESS handoff
- (b) `qcohSectionsAwayLocalized` (`def:qcoh_sections_localized`) — tilde-M case
  via `tilde.toOpen`/`tilde.toOpen_res`; this is independent of (a).
- (c) `sectionCech_homology_exact` (`lem:section_cech_homology_exact`) — needs the
  `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PresheafCech`, then identify
  `sectionCechComplex` degreewise with `D•` (the `dCoeff`/`dDiff` above) and apply
  `exactAt_iff_isZero_homology` + `ShortComplex.moduleCat_exact_iff` to turn
  `dDiff_exact` into `IsZero (homology p)`.
- (d) `sectionCech_affine_vanishing` (`lem:cech_acyclic_affine` §section form) —
  assemble (a)–(c).

## Dead ends / cautions
- Do NOT route step (a) through a "localization preserves exactness" lemma applied
  to `Function.Exact (dDiff …)` — that is circular (it IS what (a) proves). The
  only non-circular direction is `cechLocalized_exact` (localised, already proven)

- `s_r * sprod σ` vs `sprod σ * s r`: the canonical `IsLocalizedModule` instance is
  for the literal `cechCoeff` element `s r * sprod σ`; applying
  `comparison_isLocalizedModule` (whose `fb` instance is at `a*b`) needs an explicit
  `haveI … := by rw [mul_comm]; infer_instance` (see `dToCech_isLocalizedModule`).

## Needs blueprint entry (lean_aux nodes — restore 1-to-1 next iter)
All 10 new non-private decls are infrastructure with no blueprint block yet. Bundle
into the `\lean{...}` list of `lem:cech_acyclic_affine` (or a new
`def:section_cech_module_complex` block):

funext σ
  simp only [CombinatorialCech.depDiff, dDiff_apply, map_sum]
  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => ?_
  rw [map_zsmul, cechCoface_dToCech]

/-! ### Assembly of step (a): positive-degree exactness of `D•`

The localised differential at a fixed index `r`, bundled as an `R`-linear map
(`locDiff`), so that `IsLocalizedModule.map` uniqueness identifies it with the
localisation of `dDiff`.  Everything here is at a *fixed* index `r : ι` (no
`Classical.choose`), hence friction-free; the spanning-element bookkeeping is
confined to the final `dDiff_exact`. -/

/-- R-linear underlying map of the localised coface `cechCoface` (the `comparison`
before `.toAddMonoidHom`). -/
noncomputable def cechCofaceLin (r : ι) (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
    cechCoeff s M r (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →ₗ[R] cechCoeff s M r σ :=
  comparison
    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s (σ ∘ j.succAbove))) M)
    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M)
    (Inverts.of_dvd (mul_dvd_mul_left (s r) (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ j))
      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (s r * sprod s σ)) M))

lemma cechCoface_apply (r : ι) (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 1))
    (x : cechCoeff s M r (σ ∘ j.succAbove)) :
    cechCoface s M r m σ j x = cechCofaceLin s M r m σ j x := rfl

/-- The localised section Čech differential at index `r`, as a bundled `R`-linear
map (mirrors `dDiff` with the localised cofaces). -/
noncomputable def locDiff (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
    (∀ σ : Fin m → ι, cechCoeff s M r σ) →ₗ[R] (∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, cechCoeff s M r σ) :=
  LinearMap.pi fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (m + 1),
    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • (cechCofaceLin s M r m σ j ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj (σ ∘ j.succAbove))

lemma locDiff_apply (r : ι) (m : ℕ) (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, cechCoeff s M r σ)
    (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
    locDiff s M r m t σ
      = ∑ j : Fin (m + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • cechCofaceLin s M r m σ j (t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)) := by
  rw [locDiff, LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.sum_apply]
  exact Finset.sum_congr rfl fun d _ => rfl

/-- `locDiff` agrees, as a raw function, with the localised combinatorial
differential `CombinatorialCech.depDiff (cechCoface)` already shown exact. -/
lemma locDiff_eq_depDiff (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
    ⇑(locDiff s M r m)
      = CombinatorialCech.depDiff (A := fun _ σ => cechCoeff s M r σ) (cechCoface s M r) (m := m) := by
  funext t σ
  rw [locDiff_apply]
  simp only [CombinatorialCech.depDiff]
  exact Finset.sum_congr rfl fun j _ => by rw [cechCoface_apply]

/-- Positive-degree exactness of the bundled localised differential, transported
from `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`. -/
lemma locDiff_exact (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
    Function.Exact (locDiff s M r (m + 1)) (locDiff s M r (m + 2)) := by
  rw [locDiff_eq_depDiff, locDiff_eq_depDiff]
  exact CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact s M r m

/-- The product localisation map `D^m → ∏_σ M_{s_r·s_σ}` at index `r`. -/
noncomputable def fLoc (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
    (∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) →ₗ[R] (∀ σ : Fin m → ι, cechCoeff s M r σ) :=
  LinearMap.pi fun σ => dToCech s M r σ ∘ₗ LinearMap.proj σ

lemma fLoc_apply (r : ι) (m : ℕ) (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) (σ : Fin m → ι) :
    fLoc s M r m t σ = dToCech s M r σ (t σ) := by
  simp only [fLoc, LinearMap.pi_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.proj_apply]

/-- `fLoc` exhibits the product localised coefficient as the localisation of `D^m`
away from `s_r` (from `IsLocalizedModule.pi` + `dToCech_isLocalizedModule`). -/
lemma fLoc_isLocalizedModule [Finite ι] (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
    IsLocalizedModule.Away (s r) (fLoc s M r m) := by
  haveI : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (s r)) (dToCech s M r σ) :=
    fun σ => dToCech_isLocalizedModule s M r σ
  exact IsLocalizedModule.pi (Submonoid.powers (s r)) fun σ => dToCech s M r σ

/-- The localised differential intertwines `fLoc` with `dDiff` (the `dToCech_comm`
square, packaged through the bundled maps). -/
lemma locDiff_fLoc (r : ι) (m : ℕ) (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, dCoeff s M σ) :
    locDiff s M r m (fLoc s M r m t) = fLoc s M r (m + 1) (dDiff s M m t) := by
  have h1 : ⇑(locDiff s M r m) (fLoc s M r m t)
      = CombinatorialCech.depDiff (A := fun _ σ => cechCoeff s M r σ) (cechCoface s M r) (m := m)
          (fun σ => dToCech s M r σ (t σ)) := by
    rw [locDiff_eq_depDiff]; congr 1; funext σ; exact fLoc_apply s M r m t σ
  rw [h1, dToCech_comm]
  funext σ; rw [fLoc_apply]

/-- `IsLocalizedModule.map` of `dDiff` is the bundled localised differential
`locDiff` — by `IsLocalizedModule.ext`, the comparison reducing to `locDiff_fLoc`. -/
lemma map_dDiff_eq_locDiff [Finite ι] (r : ι) (m : ℕ) :
    IsLocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (s r)) (fLoc s M r m) (fLoc s M r (m + 1))
        (dDiff s M m) = locDiff s M r m := by
  haveI := fLoc_isLocalizedModule s M r m
  haveI := fLoc_isLocalizedModule s M r (m + 1)
  apply IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers (s r)) (fLoc s M r m)
    (fun x => IsLocalizedModule.map_units (fLoc s M r (m + 1)) x)
  apply LinearMap.ext; intro t
  rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, IsLocalizedModule.map_apply, locDiff_fLoc]

end SectionCechModule

/-- **Step (a): positive-degree exactness of the un-localised section Čech module
complex `D•`** (`lem:cech_acyclic_affine`, the `R`-module core).  For a spanning
family `s : ι → R` (`Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤`), the complex
`∏_σ M_{s_σ}` is exact in positive degrees.  Reduced — via
`exact_of_isLocalized_span` localising at each spanning element `s_r` — to the
exactness `locDiff_exact` of the localised complexes (which is the dependent
combinatorial core `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`), through the localised-
differential identification `map_dDiff_eq_locDiff`. -/
lemma dDiff_exact [Finite ι] (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (m : ℕ) :
    Function.Exact (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2)) := by
  classical
  refine exact_of_isLocalized_span (Set.range s) hs
    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, cechCoeff s M ρ.2.choose σ)
    (fun ρ => fLoc s M ρ.2.choose (m + 1))
    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι, cechCoeff s M ρ.2.choose σ)
    (fun ρ => fLoc s M ρ.2.choose (m + 2))
    (fun ρ => ∀ σ : Fin (m + 3) → ι, cechCoeff s M ρ.2.choose σ)
    (fun ρ => fLoc s M ρ.2.choose (m + 3))
    (dDiff s M (m + 1)) (dDiff s M (m + 2)) (fun ρ => ?_)
  · -- the away element ↑ρ is the spanning element s (ρ.2.choose)
    have hρ : (ρ.2.choose : ι) ∈ Set.range s := ⟨ρ.2.choose, rfl⟩
    rw [show (↑ρ : R) = s ρ.2.choose from ρ.2.choose_spec.symm,
      map_dDiff_eq_locDiff, map_dDiff_eq_locDiff]
    exact locDiff_exact s M ρ.2.choose m
  -- the `IsLocalizedModule.Away ↑ρ` instances for the three localisation families
  all_goals
    intro ρ
    rw [show (↑ρ : R) = s ρ.2.choose from ρ.2.choose_spec.symm]
    infer_instance

end SectionCechModule

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAcyclic.lean

## Session summary (iter-019, mathlib-build, P3 L1 lane)

**Landed the named section-form target step (a): `dDiff_exact`** — positive-degree
exactness of the un-localised section Čech module complex `D• = ∏_σ M_{s_σ}`, the
`R`-module core of `lem:cech_acyclic_affine`. Built **24 axiom-clean declarations**
bottom-up (incl. the planner's flagged keystone gap — localisation transitivity).
All `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (the "opaque" source-
scan flag is just the word in `spanIdx`'s docstring). File compiles error-free;
sorry count **1 → 1** (only the pre-existing superseded relative-form
`CechAcyclic.affine`, line 74, untouched).

### `namespace AwayComparison`
- `Inverts.smul_pow_cancel` — a power of an invertibly-acting element acts
  injectively on a localisation.
- **`comparison_isLocalizedModule`** — **KEYSTONE: localisation transitivity.**
  If `Ma` localises `M` away from `a` and `Mb` away from `a*b`, the comparison
  `Ma → Mb` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers b)` (`M_a[1/b] = M_{ab}`). Proved via the
  `IsLocalizedModule.mk` constructor. **This was the planner's named remaining gap.**

### `namespace SectionCechModule` — the complex `D•` and step (a)
- `dCoeff {m} (σ) : Type u` (abbrev) `= LocalizedModule (powers (sprod s σ)) M`.
- `dCoface (m) (σ) (j)` — un-localised coface comparison (R-linear `→ₗ`).
- `dDiff (m)` — un-localised differential, bundled `LinearMap` (`LinearMap.pi` of
  alternating-signed composed `LinearMap.proj`); `dDiff_apply` its sum form.
- `dToCech (r) {m} (σ)` — per-index comparison `M_{s_σ} → M_{s_r·s_σ}`.
- **`dToCech_isLocalizedModule`** — `IsLocalizedModule.Away (s r) (dToCech …)`
  (payoff of the keystone; commutativity by `rw [mul_comm]; infer_instance`).
- **`cechCoface_dToCech`** — per-coface naturality square (via `comparison_comp_apply`).
- **`dToCech_comm`** — differential-naturality square: localising `dDiff` via
  `dToCech` gives `depDiff (cechCoface)` (sums `cechCoface_dToCech`).
- `cechCofaceLin`, `cechCoface_apply` — R-linear underlying map of `cechCoface`.
- `locDiff (r) (m)` — bundled localised differential; `locDiff_apply`,
  `locDiff_eq_depDiff` (`⇑locDiff = CombinatorialCech.depDiff (cechCoface)`),
  `locDiff_exact` (transports `CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`).
- `fLoc (r) (m)` — product localisation map `D^m → ∏_σ M_{s_r·s_σ}`; `fLoc_apply`;
  `instance fLoc_isLocalizedModule [Finite ι]` (via `IsLocalizedModule.pi`).
- `locDiff_fLoc` — `locDiff ∘ fLoc = fLoc ∘ dDiff` (bundled `dToCech_comm`).
- `map_dDiff_eq_locDiff` — `IsLocalizedModule.map (powers a) (fLoc r m) (fLoc r (m+1))
  (dDiff m) = locDiff r m` (via `IsLocalizedModule.ext`; away element `a` left
  general with the localisation instances as hypotheses, so the final assembly needs
  no `↑ρ`-rewrite inside the map).
- `spanIdx` / `spanIdx_spec` (private) — opaque chosen `ι`-index for a spanning-set
  element (keeps the `dDiff_exact` rewrite motive type-correct).
- **`dDiff_exact [Finite ι] (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (m)`** —
  `Function.Exact (dDiff s M (m+1)) (dDiff s M (m+2))`. Via
  `exact_of_isLocalized_span (Set.range s) hs`, localising at each spanning element
  and discharging each node with `map_dDiff_eq_locDiff` + `locDiff_exact`.

## Why I stopped — `Real progress`

24 axiom-clean declarations, culminating in the **named step-(a) target
`dDiff_exact`**. This is the complete `R`-module exactness core of
`lem:cech_acyclic_affine`. I stopped here because step (a) is done and steps (b)–(d)
are a distinct sub-build (sheaf-section identification, not module algebra) better
handled as the next lane. No approaches were left un-attempted.

## Next steps (b)–(d) — precise handoff
- **(b) `qcohSectionsAwayLocalized`** (`def:qcoh_sections_localized`) — independent of
  (a). Tilde-`M` case via `tilde.toOpen` (`IsLocalizedModule (.powers g)`),
  `tilde.toOpen_res`; bolt on arbitrary-quasicoherent `F ≅ tilde(ΓF)` (Stacks 01I8)
  separately.
- **(c) `sectionCech_homology_exact`** (`lem:section_cech_homology_exact`) — add
  `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PresheafCech`; identify `sectionCechComplex`
  degreewise with `D•` (this session's `dCoeff`/`dDiff`); then
  `exactAt_iff_isZero_homology` + `ShortComplex.moduleCat_exact_iff` turn
  **`dDiff_exact`** into `IsZero (homology p)`. **`dDiff_exact` is the exact
  `Function.Exact` input this step consumes.**
- **(d) `sectionCech_affine_vanishing`** (`lem:cech_acyclic_affine` §section form) —
  assemble (a)–(c).

## Dead ends / cautions (for next prover)
- Do NOT prove (a) by "localisation preserves exactness" applied to
  `Function.Exact (dDiff …)` — circular. Only `cechLocalized_exact` (localised,
  proven) → un-localised via `exact_of_isLocalized_span` is non-circular.
- `s_r * sprod σ` vs `sprod σ * s r`: the canonical `IsLocalizedModule` instance is
  for the literal `cechCoeff` element; bridge with `rw [mul_comm]; infer_instance`
  (see `dToCech_isLocalizedModule`).
- `exact_of_isLocalized_span`'s `[∀ r, IsLocalizedModule.Away ↑r (f r)]` are
  instance-implicit (synthesised, NOT goals) — supply via `haveI inst : ∀ ρ, …`
  BEFORE the `refine`.
- Per-`ρ` index from `Set.range s` must be an OPAQUE def (`spanIdx`) or the
  `rw [s i = ↑ρ]` motive is ill-typed (`ρ.2.choose` depends on `↑ρ`). And keep the
  away element general in `map_dDiff_eq_locDiff` so the localised `map`'s instance
  arg (which mentions `↑ρ`) is never rewritten.

## Needs blueprint entry (lean_aux nodes — restore 1-to-1 next iter)
All non-private decls below are new infrastructure with no blueprint block; bundle
into the `\lean{...}` list of `lem:cech_acyclic_affine` (or a new
`def:section_cech_module_complex` block). `dDiff_exact` should be marked as the
section-form step-(a) realisation:
- `AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.smul_pow_cancel`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_isLocalizedModule`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dCoeff`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dCoface`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff_apply`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dToCech`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dToCech_isLocalizedModule`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.cechCoface_dToCech`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dToCech_comm`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.cechCofaceLin`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.cechCoface_apply`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_apply`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_eq_depDiff`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_exact`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.fLoc`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.fLoc_apply`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.fLoc_isLocalizedModule`  (instance)
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_fLoc`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.map_dDiff_eq_locDiff`
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact`  ← step (a) named target
(`spanIdx`, `spanIdx_spec` are private; no blueprint node needed.)

Key Mathlib facts relied on: `exact_of_isLocalized_span`, `IsLocalizedModule.pi`,
`IsLocalizedModule.map`/`map_apply`/`ext`/`mk'_cancel'`/`eq_iff_exists`,
`CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact`, `Module.End.isUnit_iff`.

### Handoff

`task_results/CechAcyclic.md` records all 24 decls (with the blueprint `\lean{}` list to restore the DAG), the precise next steps (b)–(d) — where step (c) `sectionCech_homology_exact` consumes `dDiff_exact` directly via `exactAt_iff_isZero_homology` + `moduleCat_exact_iff` — and the dead-ends/cautions (instance-implicit localisation families must be `haveI`'d before `refine`; the per-ρ index must be an opaque `def`; keep the away element general in `map_dDiff_eq_locDiff` so the localised map's instance arg is never rewritten).

Active prover mode: **mathlib-build**

## Your goal

Build project-local Mathlib infrastructure axiom-clean, one step at a time, going **as far as possible** in this iteration. Your output consists of definitions and lemmas that compile without `sorry` and whose `#print axioms` shows only `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. You stop only when you hit a genuine mathematical blocker — not because one step is hard.

## Invariant

**No `sorry` in your output.** Every declaration you add must be fully proved. If you cannot close the current step: try alternatives first, then either prove a smaller sub-step or leave the declaration absent. Never add a declaration with `sorry`.

## Workflow

### 1. Orient

- Read `PROGRESS.md` for the objective: what infrastructure is needed, and why.
- Read the relevant blueprint chapter. It often contains the mathematical path to the ingredient you're building.
- Read `task_pending.md` for prior dead ends. Do not repeat them.

### 2. Scout the Mathlib API

Before writing any Lean, search for what already exists:

- `lean_local_search` for related names.
- `lean_leansearch` with a description of the mathematical content.
- `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns.
- Look for near-misses: a lemma that covers a sub-case, an instance you can compose, a `simp` set that contains what you need.

Even if you think you know the API — search anyway. Signatures change, instances disappear.

### 3. Build bottom-up, step by step

Start from the deepest missing piece identified in your scouting. For each step:

1. **Draft the type signature** and check it typechecks via `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lean_run_code` before attempting the body.
2. **Write the proof.** Use Mathlib lemmas you verified exist. If a sub-step is missing, recurse: prove that sub-step first.
3. **Verify axiom-cleanliness** immediately after the step compiles: `#print axioms MyLemma`. Any `sorryAx` in the ancestry means the step is not axiom-clean — do not proceed until it is gone.
4. **Continue to the next step** in the chain.

Push as far as you can. Each axiom-clean step you add is permanent progress.

### 4. When stuck on a step — try before stopping

Before declaring a step impossible:

1. **Try a different proof route.** Reformulate the statement, weaken to a special case you can prove, use a detour through an equivalent form.
2. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 3.
3. **Search more broadly.** The lemma you need might exist under a different name or in a namespace you haven't checked.
4. **Prove a strictly smaller sub-step** that is axiom-clean and genuinely useful — something that shrinks the remaining gap.

Only after exhausting these alternatives do you stop. **Comments describing an approach you haven't tried are not exhausted alternatives — attempt them first.**

### 5. Stopping

**Before stopping, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote in a comment, TODO, or log entry? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there adjacent declarations in the chain I could attempt with the infrastructure I just built?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before declaring the step impossible?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped? Attempt it — a failed attempt with a named error is better than a comment.

Stop only when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable." **Writing a comment about a possible approach counts as identifying it — which means you should attempt it.**

**You are fully authorized to make every mathematical and structural decision within your assigned file without waiting for the planner.** "I'll let the planner decide which route to take" is never a valid stop reason — pick the most promising route, attempt it, document the outcome. The planner corrects route choices next iter; an idle session corrects nothing.

Stop when you have tried alternatives and cannot make further axiom-clean progress. Before stopping:

- Commit all axiom-clean steps you completed.
- Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with a precise handoff (see Logging below).
- Ensure the file compiles with no `sorry`.

## File structure

Place new declarations under a clearly delimited section:

```lean
/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — <TopicName> -/
```

- Use `private` for helpers with no downstream use outside this file.
- Use `theorem` / `lemma` (non-private) for steps that other files will import.
- Add a one-line docstring to each non-private declaration explaining why it is project-local.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any existing declaration. You may add new declarations freely. You must not rename, re-type, or modify signatures of protected declarations.

## API alignment

Mathlib names and signatures are the most common failure source.

- Verify every name with `lean_local_search` or `lean_loogle` — do not rely on memory.
- Check that typeclass assumptions in your statement match what Mathlib requires.
- For `def`-backed types: Lean may not unfold them for typeclass synthesis; use explicit coercions or `change` / `show` in the proof body.

## Write permissions

Only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror the `.lean` path):

```markdown
# Algebra/MathlibSupplement.lean

## Session summary
- Built axiom-clean: `Foo.bar`, `Foo.baz` (lines 12–40)
- Blocked on: `Foo.qux` — needs `Bar.aux` which requires [precise statement]

## Foo.bar (line 12)
- **Approach:** Direct from `Mathlib.Algebra.X` via `simp [Y]`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean

## Foo.qux (not added)
- **Approach 1:** Tried [route] — FAILED because [reason]
- **Approach 2:** Tried informal agent — suggested [sketch], formalization blocked on [specific gap]
- **Next step:** Build `Bar.aux : [precise type statement]` first
- **Dead end:** Do not retry [route] — [why it fails]
```

One section per declaration attempted. For each: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / NOT ADDED), dead-end warnings, next step.

## End-of-session handoff

**Write a `## Summary` section** before the verdicts: declarations added (count + names), declarations blocked (count + why), sorry count before → after across your file.

**Write a `## Needs blueprint entry` section.** This mode is the biggest source of blueprint debt: every non-private definition/lemma you add is, by construction, infrastructure with **no blueprint block yet** — a `lean_aux` node invisible to the dependency graph (`archon dag-query unmatched` will list it) until the planner/reviewer blueprints it. List each new non-private declaration (name, file, and the facts its proof relies on) so the 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence the dag agent built is restored next iter. Closing 0 project sorries while adding 3 unreported helpers does not advance the project — it quietly re-introduces the isolation the DAG phase worked to eliminate. Reporting them is mandatory.

**Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest. Only axiom-clean Lean declarations count as progress. Comments, sketches, and log prose do NOT. If you wrote down approaches you didn't attempt, say so:

- `Real progress`: N axiom-clean declarations added — name each one with its line number.
- `Partial progress`: added some declarations, hit a specific blocker on the next — name the blocker precisely (not "it's complex", but the exact type or missing ingredient).
- `Approaches written but not attempted`: identified routes in comments or log but did not attempt them — name each. This is valid but weak; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
- `Blocked — alternatives exhausted`: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), alternative routes tried, all failed — name what was tried, why each failed, and the exact type statement of the next needed ingredient.
- `Infrastructure already exists`: Mathlib has what was needed — cite the exact lemma name and namespace.
