184	/-- **The free-presheaf Čech complex of a finite open cover** (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`).
185	
186	The chain complex `K(𝒰)_•` in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is
187	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
188	`= ⨁_σ (PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`, with the
189	alternating-sum index-dropping differential. Built as the `alternatingFaceMapComplex` of
190	`cechFreeSimplicial`, so `d² = 0` holds by the simplicial identities.
191	
192	Project-local: the free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
193	noncomputable def cechFreePresheafComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
194	    ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ :=
195	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex X.PresheafOfModules).obj (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰)
196	
197	/-- Degreewise unfolding of the free Čech complex: the degree-`p` term is the coproduct of
198	free presheaves over the `(p+1)`-fold multi-indices. Exposed (it holds by `rfl`) so that the
199	downstream hom-identification and quasi-isomorphism arguments can rewrite the degree-`p`
200	object without unfolding `alternatingFaceMapComplex` and `cechFreeSimplicial` by hand. -/
201	lemma cechFreePresheafComplex_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ) :
202	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p
203	      = ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) :=
204	  rfl
205	
206	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — augmentation and cover structure presheaf -/
207	
208	/-- **Augmentation of a representable free presheaf onto the structure presheaf.**
209	
210	The canonical map `freeYoneda V ⟶ unit` (= `O_X`) corresponding under the free–Yoneda hom
211	bijection to the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)`. Concretely it sends the free generator over a
212	`W ⊆ V` to `1 ∈ O_X(W)`. This is the per-summand component of the Čech augmentation
213	`K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰`.
214	
215	Project-local: Mathlib has neither `freeYoneda` nor its augmentation. -/
216	noncomputable def freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
217	    freeYoneda.obj V ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
218	  (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).symm
219	    (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)))
220	
221	/-- Value of the free–Yoneda hom bijection on the augmentation: it is the unit section `1`. -/
222	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
223	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj) (freeYonedaAug V)
224	      = (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))) := by
225	  simp only [freeYonedaAug, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
226	
227	/-- Value of the augmentation `freeYoneda V' ⟶ O_X` on a generator `freeMk g` (`g : V ⟶ V'`):
228	it is the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)` (the restriction of `1 ∈ O_X(V')`). -/
229	private lemma freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (g : V ⟶ V') :
230	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk g)
231	      = (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) := by
232	  have key := (freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply V' (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
233	      (freeYonedaAug V')).symm.trans (freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug V')
234	  have hmap : (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYoneda.obj V').map g.op))
235	      (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V')) = ModuleCat.freeMk g := by
236	    erw [PresheafOfModules.freeObj_map, ModuleCat.freeDesc_apply]
237	    change ModuleCat.freeMk ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((yoneda.obj V').map g.op)) (𝟙 V'))
238	      = ModuleCat.freeMk g
239	    congr 1
240	  have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply (freeYonedaAug V') g.op
241	    (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))
242	  rw [hmap] at hnat
243	  rw [hnat]
244	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op))
245	    ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V'))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))) = 1
246	  exact (congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op)) key).trans (map_one _)
247	
248	/-- **Naturality of the augmentation.** For `V ≤ V'` the restriction map of free presheaves
249	composed with the augmentation onto `O_X` equals the augmentation over the smaller open:
250	`freeYoneda.map (incl) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V`. This is the cocone condition
251	that turns the per-summand augmentations into a chain map `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
252	lemma freeYoneda_map_comp_aug {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : V ≤ V') :
253	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE h) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V := by
254	  apply (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).injective
255	  rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app]
256	  erw [ModuleCat.comp_apply, Functor.comp_map,
257	    PresheafOfModules.free_map_app, ModuleCat.free_map_apply, freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk]
258	
259	/-- **Degree-`0` augmentation of the free Čech complex.** The map
260	`K(𝒰)_0 = ∐_{σ : Fin 1 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X` assembled from the per-summand
261	augmentations `freeYonedaAug`. Its image presheaf is the cover structure presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
262	noncomputable def cechFreeAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
263	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X 0 ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
264	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
265	
266	/-- **The cover structure presheaf** `O_𝒰` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
267	
268	The image (as a presheaf of `O_X`-modules) of the degree-`0` augmentation `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_X`
269	(`cechFreeAug`). Concretely `O_𝒰(W) = O_X(W)` when `W` is contained in some cover member
270	`U_i`, and `0` otherwise. The free Čech complex `K(𝒰)_•` is a resolution of `O_𝒰`
271	(`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`).
272	
273	Project-local: the image presheaf of a cover's augmentation has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
274	noncomputable def coverStructurePresheaf (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
275	    X.PresheafOfModules :=
276	  Limits.image (cechFreeAug 𝒰)
277	
278	/-- Each face map `δ i : K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation is the same
279	multi-indexed augmentation `∐_{σ : Fin 2 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X`, independent of `i`.
280	This is the cosimplicial-identity input to `d ≫ aug = 0`: the two faces become equal after
281	augmenting, by naturality of `freeYonedaAug`. -/

283	    (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰
284	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun σ : Fin 2 → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by

293	(`cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`). This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
294	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/

/-- **The cosimplicial natural isomorphism `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.**

Assembles the per-degree isomorphisms `homCechSectionIsoApp` into a natural isomorphism of
cosimplicial abelian groups. The naturality square reduces, via `homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π`
in both degrees, to the single naturality square `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality` of the
free–Yoneda section identification.

Project-local: the cosimplicial comparison of the hom-complex with the section complex has no
Mathlib counterpart. -/
noncomputable def homCechSectionCosimplicialIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechCosimplicial (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
  NatIso.ofComponents (homCechSectionIsoApp 𝒰 F) (by
    intro n m f
    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
    intro σ
    sorry)

/-
Remaining assembly (handed off — see task_results): the cosimplicial natural isomorphism
`homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechCosimplicial (coverOpen 𝒰) F` via
`NatIso.ofComponents (homCechSectionIsoApp 𝒰 F)`, whose per-`σ` naturality square reduces — by
`Limits.Pi.hom_ext`, `homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π` (both degrees), `Functor.rightOp_map`,
`Limits.Sigma.ι_desc`, `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality`, and `Limits.Pi.lift_π` — to the
identity `Y.map (Sigma.ι (σ∘f)).op ≫ e_{σ∘f}.hom ≫ F.presheaf.map h.op` on both sides
(`h = homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 f.toOrderHom σ)`); then
`cechComplex_hom_identification := (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab).mapIso (that NatIso)`.
This was held back this iteration only because the upstream `FreePresheafComplex.lean` was being
edited concurrently and would not compile, so the multi-step `erw`-sensitive naturality proof

end AlgebraicGeometry
input.new_string:
/-- **The Čech hom-identification** (blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`).

The cochain-complex isomorphism `Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)` identifying the
hom-complex of the free Čech resolution with the section Čech complex of `F`. Obtained by
applying the alternating-coface-map complex functor to the cosimplicial natural isomorphism
`homCechSectionCosimplicialIso`, so the differential intertwining is automatic from the
cosimplicial naturality.

Project-local: the comparison of the free-resolution hom-complex with the section complex has
no Mathlib counterpart. -/
noncomputable def cechComplex_hom_identification (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
    homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).mapIso (homCechSectionCosimplicialIso 𝒰 F)

end AlgebraicGeometry

## homCechSectionCosimplicialIso (line 205) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Type:** `homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechCosimplicial (coverOpen 𝒰) F`.
- **Approach:** `NatIso.ofComponents (homCechSectionIsoApp 𝒰 F)` with the naturality square proved exactly along the handoff recipe (lines 196–207 comment). After `Limits.Pi.hom_ext` + `intro σ`:
  1. `dsimp only [sectionCechCosimplicial]` — **critical**: rewrites the shared middle object `(sectionCechCosimplicial …).obj m` to the literal `∏ᶜ`/`piObj`. Without this, `Category.assoc`/`rw` cannot match the triple composition (the object is defeq-but-not-syntactic, classic carrier mismatch).
  2. `rw [Category.assoc, Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π]` reassociates and evaluates the section differential.
  3. `erw [homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π 𝒰 F m σ]` (LHS) and `erw [homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π 𝒰 F n (σ ∘ ⇑f.toOrderHom)]` (RHS, after `conv_rhs => rw [← Category.assoc]`). `erw` is required because the π is indexed by `⨅ k, coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)` in the goal vs `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` in the lemma (defeq, non-syntactic).
  4. `erw [Category.assoc]` then `erw [← freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le …)) F]` discharges the single naturality square. `erw` needed: the `homOfLE ⋯` proof term differs (proof-irrelevant defeq).
  5. `dsimp only [homCechCosimplicial, Functor.comp_map, Functor.rightOp_map]` unfolds `map f` to `(preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).map f.op).op`.
  6. `erw [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]` combines the two `preadditiveYoneda.obj F`-images on each side. `erw` needed throughout (the Yoneda hom-group carrier objects are defeq-non-syntactic).
  7. `congr 1; congr 1; apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; simp only [unop_comp]; dsimp only [cechFreeSimplicial]; erw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]; rfl` reduces to the underlying `PMod` morphism equality `Sigma.ι σ ≫ Sigma.desc(…) = freeYoneda.map h ≫ Sigma.ι (σ∘f)` and closes by `Sigma.ι_desc` + proof-irrelevance `rfl`.
- **KEY LESSON (dead-end avoidance):** Plain `rw [Category.assoc]` / `simp only [Category.assoc]` **fail silently** here ("pattern not found"/"no progress") because every composition's shared object is definitionally-but-not-syntactically equal (`(F.obj …)` vs unfolded product, Yoneda hom-group carriers, `homOfLE` proof terms). The whole proof MUST use `erw` for every associativity / functor-map-comp / naturality step, with one upfront `dsimp only [sectionCechCosimplicial]` to expose the product. Do not retry with `rw`/`simp`.

## cechComplex_hom_identification (line 240) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean — **NAMED TARGET**
- **Type:** `homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F`.
- **Approach:** `(AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).mapIso (homCechSectionCosimplicialIso 𝒰 F)` — one-liner, exactly per recipe. Both `homCechComplex` and `sectionCechComplex` are by-definition the alternating-coface complex of their cosimplicial objects, so `mapIso` of the cosimplicial iso has precisely the required type and intertwines the differentials automatically.

## injective_cech_acyclic (NOT ADDED) — blocked on Lane 2
- **Why blocked:** needs `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` (`QuasiIso (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰)`), which is **only referenced in comments** in `FreePresheafComplex.lean` (Lane 2) — no declaration exists yet (verified by grep). Build a bridging lemma: the homology of `homCechComplex 𝒰 F` is the `preadditiveYoneda.obj F`-image of the (co)homology of `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰` (i.e. `Hom(K_•, F)` exact in degree `p>0` ⇔ `Hom(-,F)` sends the augmentation quasi-iso to a quasi-iso). Even if you think you know the API — search anyway. Signatures change, instances disappear.

### 3. Build bottom-up, step by step

Start from the deepest missing piece identified in your scouting. For each step:

1. **Draft the type signature** and check it typechecks via `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lean_run_code` before attempting the body.
2. **Write the proof.** Use Mathlib lemmas you verified exist. If a sub-step is missing, recurse: prove that sub-step first.
3. **Verify axiom-cleanliness** immediately after the step compiles: `#print axioms MyLemma`. Any `sorryAx` in the ancestry means the step is not axiom-clean — do not proceed until it is gone.
4. **Continue to the next step** in the chain.

Push as far as you can. Each axiom-clean step you add is permanent progress.

### 4. When stuck on a step — try before stopping

Before declaring a step impossible:

1. **Try a different proof route.** Reformulate the statement, weaken to a special case you can prove, use a detour through an equivalent form.
2. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 3.
3. **Search more broadly.** The lemma you need might exist under a different name or in a namespace you haven't checked.
4. **Prove a strictly smaller sub-step** that is axiom-clean and genuinely useful — something that shrinks the remaining gap.

Only after exhausting these alternatives do you stop. **Comments describing an approach you haven't tried are not exhausted alternatives — attempt them first.**

### 5. Stopping

**Before stopping, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote in a comment, TODO, or log entry? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there adjacent declarations in the chain I could attempt with the infrastructure I just built?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before declaring the step impossible?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped? Attempt it — a failed attempt with a named error is better than a comment.

Stop only when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable." **Writing a comment about a possible approach counts as identifying it — which means you should attempt it.**

**You are fully authorized to make every mathematical and structural decision within your assigned file without waiting for the planner.** "I'll let the planner decide which route to take" is never a valid stop reason — pick the most promising route, attempt it, document the outcome. The planner corrects route choices next iter; an idle session corrects nothing.

Stop when you have tried alternatives and cannot make further axiom-clean progress. Before stopping:

- Commit all axiom-clean steps you completed.
- Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with a precise handoff (see Logging below).
- Ensure the file compiles with no `sorry`.

## File structure

Place new declarations under a clearly delimited section:

```lean
/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — <TopicName> -/
```

- Use `private` for helpers with no downstream use outside this file.
- Use `theorem` / `lemma` (non-private) for steps that other files will import.
- Add a one-line docstring to each non-private declaration explaining why it is project-local.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any existing declaration. You may add new declarations freely. You must not rename, re-type, or modify signatures of protected declarations.

## API alignment

Mathlib names and signatures are the most common failure source.

- Verify every name with `lean_local_search` or `lean_loogle` — do not rely on memory.
- Check that typeclass assumptions in your statement match what Mathlib requires.
- For `def`-backed types: Lean may not unfold them for typeclass synthesis; use explicit coercions or `change` / `show` in the proof body.

## Write permissions

Only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror the `.lean` path):

```markdown
# Algebra/MathlibSupplement.lean

## Session summary
- Built axiom-clean: `Foo.bar`, `Foo.baz` (lines 12–40)
- Blocked on: `Foo.qux` — needs `Bar.aux` which requires [precise statement]

## Foo.bar (line 12)
- **Approach:** Direct from `Mathlib.Algebra.X` via `simp [Y]`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean

## Foo.qux (not added)
- **Approach 1:** Tried [route] — FAILED because [reason]
- **Approach 2:** Tried informal agent — suggested [sketch], formalization blocked on [specific gap]
- **Next step:** Build `Bar.aux : [precise type statement]` first
- **Dead end:** Do not retry [route] — [why it fails]
```

One section per declaration attempted. For each: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / NOT ADDED), dead-end warnings, next step.

## End-of-session handoff

**Write a `## Summary` section** before the verdicts: declarations added (count + names), declarations blocked (count + why), sorry count before → after across your file.

**Write a `## Needs blueprint entry` section.** This mode is the biggest source of blueprint debt: every non-private definition/lemma you add is, by construction, infrastructure with **no blueprint block yet** — a `lean_aux` node invisible to the dependency graph (`archon dag-query unmatched` will list it) until the planner/reviewer blueprints it. List each new non-private declaration (name, file, and the facts its proof relies on) so the 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence the dag agent built is restored next iter. Closing 0 project sorries while adding 3 unreported helpers does not advance the project — it quietly re-introduces the isolation the DAG phase worked to eliminate. Reporting them is mandatory.

**Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest. Only axiom-clean Lean declarations count as progress. Comments, sketches, and log prose do NOT. If you wrote down approaches you didn't attempt, say so:

- `Real progress`: N axiom-clean declarations added — name each one with its line number.
- `Partial progress`: added some declarations, hit a specific blocker on the next — name the blocker precisely (not "it's complex", but the exact type or missing ingredient).
- `Approaches written but not attempted`: identified routes in comments or log but did not attempt them — name each. This is valid but weak; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
- `Blocked — alternatives exhausted`: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), alternative routes tried, all failed — name what was tried, why each failed, and the exact type statement of the next needed ingredient.
- `Infrastructure already exists`: Mathlib has what was needed — cite the exact lemma name and namespace.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

10	/-!
11	# Free-presheaf Čech complex (P3b bridge — free-complex side)
12	
13	This file is the free-presheaf-complex side of the P3b bridge.  The section side lives in
14	`PresheafCech.lean`; this file owns the two free-complex declarations:
15	
16	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex`  (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`)
17	  — the chain complex of free presheaves of `O_X`-modules whose degree-`p` term is
18	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj`
19	  `  (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`.
20	
21	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`  (`lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`)
22	  — the free complex is a quasi-isomorphism / free resolution of `O_𝒰`.
23	-/
24	
25	universe u
26	
27	open CategoryTheory Limits
28	
29	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
30	
31	/-
32	Planner strategy (P3b free-complex side; see analogies/p3b-presheafcech.md +
33	blueprint §Presheaf-level Čech machinery):
34	
35	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36	`cechFreePresheafComplex`
37	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
38	Goal: a `ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ` whose degree-`p` term is
39	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`
40	with the alternating-face differential.
41	
42	Recommended build path — use the simplicial route so that d²=0 comes for free:
43	1. Construct a `SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules` whose `n`-simplices are the
44	   above direct sums.
45	2. Apply `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` to obtain the chain complex.
46	   This gives d²=0 automatically via `SimplicialObject.boundarySquareZero`.
47	
48	Key API:
49	- `PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj :`
50	  `  ((Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u) ⥤ PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj`
51	  — the free-presheaf-of-modules functor.  Use it as `(PresheafOfModules.free _).obj` and
52	  `(PresheafOfModules.free _).map`.
53	- `yoneda.obj V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u` — the representable presheaf of sets at `V`.
54	  Do NOT introduce a bespoke `j_!`; `free ∘ yoneda` is the correct substitute.
55	- `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` — turns a simplicial abelian group (or
56	  simplicial object in an abelian category) into a chain complex; d²=0 is a theorem.
57	- Direct sums: `⨁` is `DirectSum`; in `PresheafOfModules` use
58	  `Limits.biproduct` / `DirectSum.lof` / `DirectSum.desc`.
59	
60	DEAD END — do NOT hand-roll the alternating-sum identity for d²=0.  Use the simplicial
61	path above.
62	
63	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
64	`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
65	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
66	Goal: show that `cechFreePresheafComplex` → `O_𝒰[0]` is a quasi-isomorphism (i.e.,
67	the free complex is a free resolution of the structure sheaf restricted to the cover).
68	
69	Recommended build path — objectwise contracting homotopy:
70	1. Homology in `X.PresheafOfModules` is computed objectwise (colimits are objectwise).
71	   So it suffices to exhibit, for each open `V : Opens ↥X`, a contracting homotopy on
72	   the sectionwise complex `cechFreePresheafComplex(V)`.
73	2. Sectionwise at `V`, degree `p` is `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} R(V)` for those multi-indices
74	   `σ` with `V ⊆ ⨅ k, U (σ k)`, and `0` otherwise.
75	3. Fix any `i_fix : ι` with `V ⊆ U i_fix` (if none exists, the complex is `0`).
76	   The homotopy `h : K_p(V) → K_{p+1}(V)` maps the `σ`-summand to the `(i_fix, σ)`-summand
77	   (prepend `i_fix`).  Check `dh + hd = id` at each degree.
78	4. Package as `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy`, then use `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso`.
79	
80	Key API:
81	- `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy` — `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Homotopy`.
82	- `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso` (or `Homotopy.toQuasiIso`) — homotopy equivalence ⟹ quasi-iso.
83	
84	DEAD END — do NOT route through `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy`.  That
85	interface has a different index convention and is not directly applicable here.
86	-/
87	
88	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — free-presheaf Čech complex
89	
90	This section builds the free-presheaf Čech complex `cechFreePresheafComplex` of a finite
91	open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover` as the alternating-face-map chain complex of an explicit
92	simplicial object `cechFreeSimplicial`.  Everything is project-local: Mathlib has the
93	free-presheaf-of-modules functor, the Yoneda embedding and `alternatingFaceMapComplex`,
94	but not their assembly into the Čech free resolution of a cover.
95	
96	We require `[Finite 𝒰.I₀]` so that the index types `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` of the degreewise
97	coproducts are finite (`HasCoproductsOfShape`); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of
98	the downstream protected theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`. -/
99	
100	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
101	
102	/-- **Free presheaf of modules on a representable open.**
103	
104	The composite `Opens X --yoneda--> ((Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type) --free--> X.PresheafOfModules`.
105	For an open `V`, `freeYoneda.obj V` is the extension-by-zero free presheaf of modules
106	`(j_V)_! O_X|_V` of the blueprint: on sections over `W` it is the free `O_X(W)`-module on
107	`Hom(W, V)`, i.e. `O_X(W)` if `W ⊆ V` and `0` otherwise.
108	
109	Project-local because Mathlib provides `PresheafOfModules.free` and `yoneda` separately but
110	not this composite, which is the basic building block of the free Čech complex. -/
111	noncomputable def freeYoneda : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules :=
112	  yoneda ⋙ PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj
113	
114	/-- The open underlying the `i`-th member of an open cover, as `(𝒰.f i).opensRange`. -/
115	def coverOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (i : 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X := (𝒰.f i).opensRange
116	
117	/-- The intersection open `⨅ k, U (σ k)` indexed by a tuple `σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀`.
118	
119	For `κ = Fin (p+1)` this is the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{σ(0)…σ(p)}` indexing the
120	degree-`p` term of the Čech complex. -/
121	def coverInterOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X :=
122	  ⨅ k, coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)
123	
124	/-- Reindexing along `α : κ' → κ` enlarges the intersection open: precomposing a tuple with
125	`α` keeps a subset of the indices, so the intersection grows. This `≤` supplies the
126	representable index-dropping maps that define the Čech differential. -/
127	lemma coverInterOpen_comp_le (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ κ' : Type} (α : κ' → κ) (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
128	    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α) :=
129	  le_iInf fun k => iInf_le _ (α k)
130	
131	/-- A coproduct injection transported along an equality of indices: `eqToHom` of the induced
132	object equality cancels the index change. A generic categorical helper used to discharge the
133	dependent-index bookkeeping in the simplicial identities of `cechFreeSimplicial`. -/
134	private lemma sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport {C : Type*} [Category C] {β : Type*} (B : β → C)
135	    [HasCoproduct B] {a b : β} (e : a = b) :
136	    eqToHom (congrArg B e) ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι B b = Limits.Sigma.ι B a := by
137	  subst e; simp
138	
139	/-- **The free Čech simplicial object of a finite open cover.**
140	
141	The simplicial object in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is the coproduct
142	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and whose simplicial maps
143	reindex the multi-index `σ ↦ σ ∘ α` (along the order map of `α : [p] ⟶ [q]`) together with
144	the representable index-dropping inclusion `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α)`.
145	
146	Taking its `alternatingFaceMapComplex` produces `cechFreePresheafComplex` with `d² = 0` for
147	free (the simplicial route avoids hand-rolling the alternating-sum identity).
148	
149	Project-local: this is the simplicial backbone of the free Čech resolution; it has no
150	Mathlib counterpart. -/
151	noncomputable def cechFreeSimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
152	    SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules where
153	  obj n := ∐ fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
154	  map {n m} α := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
155	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 α.unop.toOrderHom σ)) ≫
156	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (m.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))
157	        (σ ∘ α.unop.toOrderHom)
158	  map_id n := by
159	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
160	    simp only [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Category.comp_id]
161	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) = σ := by funext i; simp
162	    have eo : coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop))
163	        = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ := by rw [e]
164	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE
165	        (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 (SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) σ)) (eqToHom eo.symm),
166	      eqToHom_map]
167	    exact sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
168	      (fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) e
169	  map_comp {n m k} f g := by
170	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
171	    simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
172	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (f ≫ g).unop)
173	        = (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f.unop))
174	            ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom g.unop) := by
175	      funext i; simp [unop_comp, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom]
176	    rw [← sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
177	          (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e,
178	      show (eqToHom (congrArg (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
179	              freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e))
180	          = freeYoneda.map (eqToHom (congrArg (coverInterOpen 𝒰) e)) from (eqToHom_map _ _).symm]
181	    simp only [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
182	    congr 2
183	
184	/-- **The free-presheaf Čech complex of a finite open cover** (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`).
185	
186	The chain complex `K(𝒰)_•` in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is
187	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
188	`= ⨁_σ (PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`, with the
189	alternating-sum index-dropping differential. Built as the `alternatingFaceMapComplex` of
190	`cechFreeSimplicial`, so `d² = 0` holds by the simplicial identities.
191	
192	Project-local: the free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
193	noncomputable def cechFreePresheafComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
194	    ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ :=
195	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex X.PresheafOfModules).obj (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰)
196	
197	/-- Degreewise unfolding of the free Čech complex: the degree-`p` term is the coproduct of
198	free presheaves over the `(p+1)`-fold multi-indices. Exposed (it holds by `rfl`) so that the
199	downstream hom-identification and quasi-isomorphism arguments can rewrite the degree-`p`
200	object without unfolding `alternatingFaceMapComplex` and `cechFreeSimplicial` by hand. -/
201	lemma cechFreePresheafComplex_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ) :
202	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p
203	      = ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) :=
204	  rfl
205	
206	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — augmentation and cover structure presheaf -/
207	
208	/-- **Augmentation of a representable free presheaf onto the structure presheaf.**
209	
210	The canonical map `freeYoneda V ⟶ unit` (= `O_X`) corresponding under the free–Yoneda hom
211	bijection to the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)`. Concretely it sends the free generator over a
212	`W ⊆ V` to `1 ∈ O_X(W)`. This is the per-summand component of the Čech augmentation
213	`K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰`.
214	
215	Project-local: Mathlib has neither `freeYoneda` nor its augmentation. -/
216	noncomputable def freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
217	    freeYoneda.obj V ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
218	  (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).symm
219	    (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)))
220	
221	/-- Value of the free–Yoneda hom bijection on the augmentation: it is the unit section `1`. -/
222	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
223	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj) (freeYonedaAug V)
224	      = (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))) := by
225	  simp only [freeYonedaAug, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
226	
227	/-- Value of the augmentation `freeYoneda V' ⟶ O_X` on a generator `freeMk g` (`g : V ⟶ V'`):
228	it is the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)` (the restriction of `1 ∈ O_X(V')`). -/
229	private lemma freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (g : V ⟶ V') :
230	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk g)
231	      = (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) := by
232	  have key := (freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply V' (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
233	      (freeYonedaAug V')).symm.trans (freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug V')
234	  have hmap : (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYoneda.obj V').map g.op))
235	      (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V')) = ModuleCat.freeMk g := by
236	    erw [PresheafOfModules.freeObj_map, ModuleCat.freeDesc_apply]
237	    change ModuleCat.freeMk ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((yoneda.obj V').map g.op)) (𝟙 V'))
238	      = ModuleCat.freeMk g
239	    congr 1
240	  have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply (freeYonedaAug V') g.op
241	    (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))
242	  rw [hmap] at hnat
243	  rw [hnat]
244	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op))
245	    ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V'))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))) = 1
246	  exact (congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op)) key).trans (map_one _)
247	
248	/-- **Naturality of the augmentation.** For `V ≤ V'` the restriction map of free presheaves
249	composed with the augmentation onto `O_X` equals the augmentation over the smaller open:
250	`freeYoneda.map (incl) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V`. This is the cocone condition
251	that turns the per-summand augmentations into a chain map `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
252	lemma freeYoneda_map_comp_aug {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : V ≤ V') :
253	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE h) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V := by
254	  apply (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).injective
255	  rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app]
256	  erw [ModuleCat.comp_apply, Functor.comp_map,
257	    PresheafOfModules.free_map_app, ModuleCat.free_map_apply, freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk]
258	
259	/-- **Degree-`0` augmentation of the free Čech complex.** The map
260	`K(𝒰)_0 = ∐_{σ : Fin 1 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X` assembled from the per-summand
261	augmentations `freeYonedaAug`. Its image presheaf is the cover structure presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
262	noncomputable def cechFreeAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
263	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X 0 ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
264	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
265	
266	/-- **The cover structure presheaf** `O_𝒰` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
267	
268	The image (as a presheaf of `O_X`-modules) of the degree-`0` augmentation `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_X`
269	(`cechFreeAug`). Concretely `O_𝒰(W) = O_X(W)` when `W` is contained in some cover member
270	`U_i`, and `0` otherwise. The free Čech complex `K(𝒰)_•` is a resolution of `O_𝒰`
271	(`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`).
272	
273	Project-local: the image presheaf of a cover's augmentation has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
274	noncomputable def coverStructurePresheaf (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
275	    X.PresheafOfModules :=
276	  Limits.image (cechFreeAug 𝒰)
277	
278	/-- Each face map `δ i : K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation is the same
279	multi-indexed augmentation `∐_{σ : Fin 2 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X`, independent of `i`.
280	This is the cosimplicial-identity input to `d ≫ aug = 0`: the two faces become equal after
281	augmenting, by naturality of `freeYonedaAug`. -/
282	private lemma cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (i : Fin 2) :
283	    (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰
284	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun σ : Fin 2 → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
285	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
286	  intro σ
287	  simp only [SimplicialObject.δ, cechFreeSimplicial, cechFreeAug, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
288	    Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.assoc]
289	  exact freeYoneda_map_comp_aug (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 _ σ)
290	
291	/-- The differential `K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation vanishes: the
292	alternating face sum `δ_0 - δ_1` is killed because both faces agree after augmenting
293	(`cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`). This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
294	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
295	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
296	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0 ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰 = 0 := by
297	  have hd : (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0
298	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0 :=
299	    AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex_obj_d (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0
300	  -- Prove the simplicial-level identity as a standalone `have` so its composition `≫` is
301	  -- elaborated fresh at the `cechFreeSimplicial.obj` types — matching
302	  -- `cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`. Rewriting `hd` into the goal first would pin the composition
303	  -- at the `.X` types of the chain complex, which are only definitionally (not syntactically)
304	  -- equal and block the rewrites.
305	  have main : AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0
306	      ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰 = 0 := by
307	    rw [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD, Fin.sum_univ_two,
308	      Preadditive.add_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp,
309	      cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug, cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug]
310	    simp only [Fin.isValue, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
311	    abel
312	  rw [hd]; exact main
313	
314	/-- The differential `K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the map onto the image presheaf
315	`O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` vanishes. Obtained from `cechFree_d_comp_aug` by cancelling the
316	mono `image.ι`. This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
317	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` (`cechFreeComplexAug`). -/
318	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
319	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0 ≫ Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰) = 0 := by
320	  rw [← cancel_mono (Limits.image.ι (cechFreeAug 𝒰)), Category.assoc, Limits.image.fac,
321	    Limits.zero_comp]
322	  exact cechFree_d_comp_aug 𝒰
323	
324	/-- **The augmentation chain map** `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
325	
326	The chain map from the free Čech complex to the cover structure presheaf concentrated in degree
327	`0`, whose degree-`0` component is the canonical map `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` onto the
328	image presheaf. The chain-map condition `d ≫ aug = 0` is `cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage`. The
329	quasi-isomorphism claim `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` asserts this map is a quasi-isomorphism.
330	
331	Project-local: the augmented free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
332	noncomputable def cechFreeComplexAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
333	    cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰 ⟶
334	      (ChainComplex.single₀ X.PresheafOfModules).obj (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰) :=
335	  ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).toSingle₀Equiv (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰)).symm
336	    ⟨Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰), cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage 𝒰⟩
337	
338	/-- The degree-`0` component of the augmentation chain map is the canonical map onto the image
339	presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
340	lemma cechFreeComplexAug_f_zero (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
341	    (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).f 0 = Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰) := by
342	  rw [cechFreeComplexAug, ChainComplex.toSingle₀Equiv_symm_apply_f_zero]
343	
344	end AlgebraicGeometry
